name: StorageUtil
description: Remember things: short-lived temp data for the current session, saved data that survives between plays, and online scoreboards.

content.text: `StorageUtil` lets your game **remember things**. There are two kinds of memory: **temp data** that lasts only for the current play session (great for passing values between scenes), and **saved data** that stays on the device so it's still there next time the player returns. It also handles online **scoreboards**.
Both kinds work like labelled boxes: you store a value under a **key** (a name) and read it back with the same key.
Temp data — for this play session
Temp data is cleared when the game stops. It's perfect for carrying a value from one scene to the next — like remembering which level the player chose.

rows: Stores a value under a name.
Reads it back — returns `default` if the key was never set.

lines: // In the menu scene, remember the chosen level.
// In the game scene, read it back (default to 1).

content.text: Saved data persists after the game closes — use it for high scores, settings, unlocked items, and progress. It works exactly like temp data, just permanent.

rows: Loads it back — returns `default` if not found.
Reserved for clearing all saved data.

lines: // Save a new high score only if it beats the old one.

content.text: To remove saved values, delete them one at a time with `DelSavedData(key)` — that's the safe, predictable way to clear what you no longer need.

Scoreboards are stored on the server and shared between all players. Because they travel over the internet, each call finishes with a **callback** — a method on your script that runs when the result arrives.

rows: `AddToScoreboard(scoreboardId, userId, name, score, maxItems, allowMultiple, customData, script, method)`
Submits a score, then calls `method` on `script` when done.
Fetches the list of scores and hands it to your callback.

lines: // Submit the player's score to the global board.
StorageUtil.AddToScoreboard("global", id, name, score, 100, false, null, this, "OnScoreSubmitted");
void OnScoreSubmitted(bool ok, int rank, string userId)

content.text: When `GetScoreboard` finishes, your callback receives whether it succeeded and a [list](/docs/uniscript/en-US/listutil) of score rows. Each row is a [dictionary](/docs/uniscript/en-US/dictionaryutil) with keys `"id"`, `"name"`, `"score"`, `"me"` (true for the current player), and `"data"` (your custom info).

rows: `string scoreboardId, string userId, string name, float score, int maxItems, bool allowMultiple, Dictionary customData, uniscript callbackScript, string callbackMethod`
`string scoreboardId, string userId, uniscript callbackScript, string callbackMethod`

content.text: Always pass a sensible `default` to `GetTempData` / `GetSavedData` so your game still works on a player's very first run, before anything has been stored.
